name: Mailchimp
description: An all-in-one email marketing platform for creating campaigns, building audiences, automating emails, and tracking results.
whyUseIt: Mailchimp is the most popular email marketing tool for small businesses. Its free tier is generous, the template editor is intuitive, and it integrates with almost everything.
aiCapabilities: AI-generated subject lines, send time optimisation, content recommendations, and predictive segmentation.
outputs: Automated email sequences (welcome series, abandoned cart, re-engagement)
A/B test results and performance reports
Campaign performance dashboards with open and click rates
Customer journey maps and automation workflows
marketerWorkflow: A marketer logs into Mailchimp to build and schedule email campaigns — designing templates, writing copy, segmenting the audience, and setting up automations like welcome sequences. They review campaign reports daily, checking open rates, click-through rates, and unsubscribe trends to optimise future sends. Weekly, they clean their list, test new subject lines, and refine automations based on subscriber behaviour data.
managerExpects: A marketing manager expects to see consistent email output — a regular newsletter cadence, functioning automations for key lifecycle stages, and growing list health metrics. They want reports showing open rates above industry benchmarks, click-through rates improving over time, and clear evidence that email is driving measurable revenue or engagement through tracked conversions.
aiWorkflowImpact: In 2025, Mailchimp's AI generates subject line variations and predicts which will perform best before you send, saving hours of manual A/B testing. Predictive segmentation automatically groups subscribers by purchase likelihood and engagement level, letting marketers target high-value segments without building complex rules manually. The AI content assistant drafts email body copy based on your campaign goal and brand tone.
demoDescription: Sign up for Mailchimp's free plan and import a small test list (even 5 email addresses you control). Build a simple welcome email using the drag-and-drop editor, set up a basic welcome automation triggered on signup, and send a test campaign to yourself to experience the full workflow from creation to inbox.

vocabulary.definition: The percentage of recipients who opened your email, used as a primary engagement metric (though less reliable since Apple's Mail Privacy Protection).
The percentage of email recipients who clicked on one or more links in your email.
Dividing your email list into smaller groups based on shared characteristics like purchase history, location, or engagement level.
A series of pre-written emails that send automatically based on triggers like signing up, making a purchase, or abandoning a cart.
The practice of regularly removing inactive, bounced, or invalid email addresses to maintain deliverability and sender reputation.
Placeholder variables (like *|FNAME|*) that dynamically insert personalised subscriber data into email content.

beginnerMistakes: Sending campaigns without segmenting the audience, blasting the same message to everyone regardless of their interests or stage
Never cleaning the email list, leading to high bounce rates and damaged sender reputation over time
Writing subject lines as afterthoughts instead of testing multiple variations to maximise open rates
Setting up automations and never reviewing their performance, letting broken or underperforming sequences run indefinitely
Ignoring mobile preview — over 60% of emails are opened on mobile, and poorly formatted emails get deleted instantly
